# Check types reference

The check types `katalyst` runs against each item, grouped by family. These pages are generated from the checks registry, so they always match the shipped engine.

## Object Check Types

Object check types validate structured frontmatter fields using schema-backed checks.

- [Object Validation]({{< relref "objects/object.md" >}}) — Validate frontmatter metadata against a named JSON Schema from schemas:.
- [Required Field]({{< relref "objects/required-field.md" >}}) — Require that a frontmatter field exists.
- [Field Type]({{< relref "objects/field-type.md" >}}) — Require that a frontmatter field has a specific type.
- [Field Enum]({{< relref "objects/field-enum.md" >}}) — Require that a field is one of a fixed set of values.
- [Number Range]({{< relref "objects/number-range.md" >}}) — Constrain a numeric field to a minimum and/or maximum value.
- [String Length]({{< relref "objects/string-length.md" >}}) — Constrain the minimum and/or maximum length of a string field.

## Markdown Check Types

Markdown check types validate relationships between frontmatter metadata and markdown body content.

- [Title Matches H1]({{< relref "markdown/title-matches-h1.md" >}}) — Require a frontmatter field to match the first H1 heading in the body.
- [Requires H1]({{< relref "markdown/requires-h1.md" >}}) — Require at least one H1 heading in the markdown body.
- [Single H1]({{< relref "markdown/single-h1.md" >}}) — Require that the markdown body contains at most one H1 heading.
- [No Heading Level Jumps]({{< relref "markdown/no-heading-level-jumps.md" >}}) — Disallow jumps larger than one heading level (for example H1 -> H3).
- [Required Section]({{< relref "markdown/required-section.md" >}}) — Require that a heading with specific text exists somewhere in the body.
- [Code Fence Language Required]({{< relref "markdown/code-fence-language-required.md" >}}) — Require that opening fenced code blocks include a language tag.

## Filesystem Check Types

Filesystem check types validate filename and path conventions for markdown items.

- [Filename Matches Slug]({{< relref "filesystem/filename-matches-slug.md" >}}) — Require a frontmatter field to match the markdown file basename.
- [Extension In]({{< relref "filesystem/extension-in.md" >}}) — Allow only specific file extensions.
- [Filename Kebab Case]({{< relref "filesystem/filename-kebab-case.md" >}}) — Require lowercase kebab-case filenames (without extension).
- [No Spaces In Path]({{< relref "filesystem/no-spaces-in-path.md" >}}) — Disallow spaces anywhere in the file path.
- [Parent Directory In]({{< relref "filesystem/parent-dir-in.md" >}}) — Require that the file's parent directory name is in an allowed set.
- [Filename Prefix]({{< relref "filesystem/filename-prefix.md" >}}) — Require that the filename starts with a specific prefix.
